subject: geography
educational_personality: exceptional classroom teacher; cream textbook; diagram teaches

lesson.big_idea: A volcano forms where molten rock rises to Earth's surface. Eruptions reshape land over time.

sections.title: Have You Wondered?
sections.body: How can a mountain suddenly pour glowing rock? Magma under pressure finds a way up.

sections.title: Using the Diagram
sections.body: Trace Magma chamber → Vent → Lava. The diagram shows the path from inside Earth to the surface.

sections.title: Concept: Volcano
sections.body: A volcano is an opening in Earth's crust that lets magma, gas, and ash escape.

sections.title: Say It Simply
sections.body: Hot melted rock rises. When it reaches the surface, we call it lava.

sections.title: In Real Life
sections.body: Ash from eruptions can travel far on the wind, affecting farms and flights hundreds of kilometres away.

sections.title: Worked Example
sections.body: Compare a quiet lava flow with an explosive ash column: gas content and magma thickness change the style.

sections.title: Try This
sections.body: Point to Vent and explain what travels through it during an eruption.

sections.title: Lesson Summary
sections.body: Volcanoes release magma and gas. They build and reshape landscapes while creating hazards people must respect.

sections.title: Think About It
sections.body: Why might people still live near volcanoes despite the risks?

sections.title: Apply Your Learning
sections.body: Draw a safety poster that uses the diagram labels to warn travellers.

diagram_package.title: Volcanoes
diagram_package.caption: Magma → Vent → Lava
diagram_package.explanation: This diagram teaches how the ideas in Volcanoes connect.
diagram_package.practice_question: Point to Magma and explain how it differs from lava.
